Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration
Off Calendar Apr-02-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Sophia Behnia (sbehnia@littler.com) of Littler Mendelson, PC for Defendants.
No other appearances.

Not argued. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTs UBER TECHNOLOGIES INC., RAISER, LLC's Motion To Compel Arbitration:

Defendants' motion to compel arbitration is granted. It is undisputed that plaintiff entered a contract containing an arbitration agreement. (Pltf. 3/24/21 Brf. 1:3-5.) After arbitration-related discovery, plaintiff argues "it is still not clear whether" the arbitration agreement "covers recruiting." (Id. at 1:10.) However, as set out in defendants' opening memorandum (12:9-13:12), the arbitration agreement delegates such arbitrability issues to the arbitrator. Plaintiff's opposition does not mention the delegation clause, much less contend that is unclear or revocable. (Tiri v. Lucky Chances, Inc. (2014) 226 Cal.App.4th 231, 239; see also Henry Schein, Inc. v. Archer & White Sales, Inc. (2019) 139 S.Ct. 524, 531 ("When the parties' contract delegates the arbitrability question to an arbitrator, the courts must respect the parties' decision as embodied in the contract.").) Plaintiff fails to demonstrate that the delegation clause itself, which was subject to the opt out provision, is unconscionable. (See Tiri, 226 Cal.App.4th at 244 ["[A]ny claim of unconscionability must be specific to the delegation clause." (emphasis in original)].)

Counsel for the defendants is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the adopted ruling.

Judge: Richard Ulmer, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration
Off Calendar Jan-29-2021 Continued To Apr-02-21 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT UBER TECHNOLOGIES INC., RAISER, LLC's Motion To Compel Arbitration.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. The hearing on defendants' motion to compel arbitration is continued to April 2, 2021 to enable completion of arbitration-related discovery. On or before March 24, 2021, plaintiff and defendants may file five-page briefs regarding the results of that discovery.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, Email: maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/RBU)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jan-13-2021 Continued To Mar-17-21 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration
Off Calendar Jan-08-2021 Continued To Jan-29-21 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT UBER TECHNOLOGIES INC., RAISER, LLC's Motion To Compel Arbitration.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. The hearing on defendants' motion to compel arbitration is continued to January 29, 2021 at 9:30 AM in Law and Motion Department 302.

Defendants' reply brief seems to raise new arguments regarding what agreement or agreements plaintiff did or did not enter. By January 15, 2021, defendants shall serve and file copies of all forms of agreement offered to drivers during 2019 and/or 2020 to the extent they have not already been filed. By January 22, 2021, plaintiff may file a five-page sur-reply directed solely to this issue.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Christine L. Jordan, CSR No. 12262, cjordan@sftc.org (302/RBU)
